pattern: For each right endpoint track the set of distinct OR values of all subarrays ending there (at most 30 distinct values due to bit-compression), then check each against k.
hints: The number of distinct OR values for subarrays ending at a fixed right index is at most 30 (one per bit), because OR can only set bits, never clear them.
Maintain a set of current OR values: for each new element, compute the OR of that element with every value in the set, plus a new subarray starting at this position.
For each OR value in the set, update the minimum |OR - k|; since OR is monotonically non-decreasing, you can break early once the OR exceeds k enough.

edge_inputs.description: no subarray hits k, closest is off by 1
OR can never decrease so long subarray may be farther
k is large but single element is closer than full OR
single bit k, array has that bit in every element

stress.description: random array with values up to 1023 (10 bits)
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = min(size, 1000)
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 1023) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(1, 1023)
    return [nums, k]
